reviews.review_description: By now I became rather reliable in guessing from reading reviews whether I will like a game or not. So here I was pretty sure that I would somehow enjoy it, bought it and put it onto the backlog some time ago. Now was the time, and wow, I was wrong. I am not only liking it, I was completely blown away. I am loving everything about this game. The concept (I always enjoyed mind bending time loops), but also the unique art style, the level design, sound and the atmospheric background music, the narrative voice-over. Even the (slightly shallow) story line helps creating the mood. The levels are designed in a way that you can develop a steady rhythm of progress. Not too hard to get stuck for too long, not too easy to make the solution obvious. Seeing yourself in action and how each step finally aligns into a (mostly) smooth flow of cooperation is extremely satisfying. Thanks for designing the game the way it is.

I really like the idea of this game where you have to work with past versions of yourself in order to complete the challenging but fun levels this is a concept that I don’t think any other vr game has so if you like a challenge that’s fun to mess around in I would definitely try this game.
I really loved it I even followed their tiktok account and I loved making my own level but it was hard so I made a few adjustments to make it easier to beat it's called "can you beat it"

For some reason, judging by the other reviews here, I seem to be the only one for whom this game is completely unplayable. Nearly every time I move my head, the image glitches so badly that I start feeling extremely nauseous within seconds. The picture literally looks like it’s being torn apart.

I tried adjusting the graphics settings to see if it would improve things, but there really isn’t much to tweak… and nothing I tried made it any better.

Why this seems to happen only to me remains a mystery — especially considering that the Quest 3 should have the exact same specs for everyone.

developer_response.body: sorry for the issues, Meta is always running a variety of experiments that sometimes break things for users even if they are on the same headset, nothing we as developers can do about it.

reviews.review_title: One of the best games in the Meta store
reviews.review_description: As far as I’m concerned, this is one of the best VR games, both for its gameplay and its stunning graphics. Kudos to the development studio!

reviews.review_title: One of the few games I really enjoy
reviews.review_description:
I don’t know why, but I found I don’t really enjoy many VR games. But I enjoy useful simulations, music-related stuff, and sports/fitness apps.

So it was a surprise that I actually enjoy this game/puzzle. What struck me was the art; it’s quite unique. And so is the gameplay. Idk what it is, but it keeps me coming back. This game is a reminder that I should continue trying games out, cuz this one is definitely really interesting. I would have easily missed this one if it didn’t have a trial.

The idea of interacting with yourself is nice, but the implementation is very bad. Three things annoy me the most: "blind" interactions, where you just do something that does not work and hope a later version of yourself will put the items in the place where you need them. And that you cannot re-record a previous record without deleting later ones. And that you cannot delete older records in order to change the order. You need to restart the whole level for that.
And the game lacks an "immediate re-record"-function.
"The last clockwinder" did a much better job creating a game around the basic concept of interacting with yourself.
Better than expected once you get into it.

reviews.review_description:
Incredible experience from start to finish. The thrill of chasing a faster and faster time on the leaderboards is unmatched by any other VR game I've played.

My only wish is for a lefty mode that swaps the starting positions of the guns and bullets since most of them were placed to be more accessible to right handed players. It's honestly not even a big deal bit I'm addicted to shaving off as many milliseconds as I can and having to fumble those around makes a difference.

And i think you guys don't often uptade the game. Also it would be nice if we would get unlimited time on recording the clones atleast. This is what sandbox truly is.

Uh good game but my brain is too simple to comprehend these puzzles, and the clones are the dumbest people ever whoever controlling them has a mental disease I bet

The game is beautiful and challenging. Took away 1 star due to frustration on the last level where it broke multiple times, didnt count the clear despite all enemies being beaten. Also there were some really interesting concepts introduced in the midgame that didnt get expanded on too much toward the end. If you're a fan over superhot you'll find a lot to like here.

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks for your review! Sorry for your bad experience with the last levels, we are looking into this! And yeah you're right, the possibilities of the clone concept are kind of endless, so we've been working hard on additional updates since launch and we just added a level creator! You can now create your own levels, go wild with cloning puzzles and play the levels of other players:)

reviews.review_description:
I have fallen in love with We Are One and while it may be lacking in narrative, it makes up for in cleverness and fun. While shooting guns is a key mechanic of the game, this is ultimately a puzzle game first and I think you should know that before diving in as it can get very hard. It might be the "smallest" clone-like I have played up to this point, but in no way does that take away from the fun I had here as it offers up new mechanics I have never had to contend with in a VR puzzler. If you are looking to play a unique title that bends your brain while manipulating time and your own actions, I highly recommend this for the asking price as it's not quite like any VR puzzler I have played up to this point.

VIDEO REVIEW - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BglXEoRxoCI

WRITTEN REVIEW - https://www.thevrgrid.com/we-are-one/

WRITTEN REVIEW -

I absolutely adore We Are One - it combines some surprisingly great VR gunplay, a great cloning mechanic, and a lot of clever puzzle design. 

Really enjoyable experience!

reviews.review_title: It's a good physics puzzle game.
reviews.review_description: This game will challenge your body coordination and your spacial thinking. It's amazing how a small company can deliver such a Polish game with great sound, graphics and mechanics. My only gripes are the underused mechanics (rebound) that reduced a sense of progression and the bland thematic story which seems to be more a marketing gimmick. It's still a good game. The recent addition of customization and sandbox will give some players a lot of pleasure. I'm looking forward for their next creation with great interest.
The mental challenge, time loop, and FPS combo is brilliant. If anyone wants to hop into a headset for the first time, I can't recommend a better game than this. All I hope for is more levels

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your kind review! 
We've added new bonus levels and recently a level editor where you can create levels and play levels of others

reviews.review_title: Very cool time loop puzzle game
reviews.review_description:
Very good puzzle shooter, if I may call it that. The basic idea of clones and time loops reminds me somewhat of "The Last Clockwinder," but it is implemented in a completely different way. Speaking of implementation, it is excellently executed, just like the game mechanics.

Surprisingly, I quite like the art style, even though I didn't find it appealing in the trailer - the style fits the game quite well. The story is okay, nothing special. It is structured in levels, with new ideas introduced every few levels.

The difficulty level ranges from very easy to quite tricky, and it's mixed throughout: an easy level can be followed by a difficult one and then another easy one. You have to think spatially and temporally to solve the puzzles, which can often be quite tricky - especially when actions need to be performed that only make sense in the future (e.g., picking up a weapon, aiming, and shooting even though it's not there yet). I also found the connected, synchronized clones to be challenging.

For puzzle, riddle, and brain-teaser fans, it's an absolute must-play. Time loop thinkers who enjoy games like "The Last Clockwinder" or "Time Stall" will also enjoy this. It's a top-notch game that I really enjoyed. The only downside is that it's quite short (please more levels!). However, there is replay value in trying to solve the levels faster and more efficiently.

reviews.review_title: Love the concept but felt underused
reviews.review_description: I played about an hour and 20 minutes of this game, I really loved the concept but in all the levels I played, it really didn't feel all that groundbreaking. The levels were just too simple. There were over 5 levels where the main gimmick was tossing a gun to yourself and shooting one enemy. I'm sure the game gets better later, but I'm not sure how much left of the game there is and I don't know if making super cool levels after over an hour of gameplay is really makes it worth it. It's like playing superhot but you just play chess for the first hour and have no use for the slowing of time.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you for your honest review! Since launch, we have continued to work on the game and have so far released 5 bonus levels and just recently added a level creator, where you can create your own levels and play the ones created by others:!

Great work team👍🍀

reviews.review_title: Great concept but room for better execut
reviews.review_description: I like the idea a lot, but the old lady pouting about dirty air or whatever endlessly is really obnoxious. This isn’t a narrative type story game, it’s a gameplay game, but I guess they needed to shoehorn in a story.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for honest review! The game includes an option called "Enable Competitive Mode" which turns off Mother Nature's voice! I hope this can help:) 
And yeah you're right, our game is highly focused on gameplay, but we still wanted to add a little story to give the game slightly more depth!
